rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARY GRACE KIMBROUGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish First Party Special Needs Trust Under Substituted Judgement; Or In The Alternative; Under Prob C 2403 And 3602(D); For Reimbursement To Petitioners; For Approval Of Caregiver Payments To Family Members; And For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/12/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Petitioners allege that the settlement agreement was approved by an Administrative Law Judge, but nothing has been filed to confirm that there was a DECISION MADE IN HEARING, as required by 20 U.S. Code Section 1415(i)(1)(A). Exhibit A is just the settlement agreement entered into by the parties. (2) 20 USC Sect. 1415(i)(3)(B) states, in part, "In any action or proceeding brought under this section, the court, in its discretion, may award reasonable attorneys' fees." Was there an order from the Administrative Law Judge for all of the legal fees shown in paragraph 7 of Exhibit F? (3) The records show that the attorney for the conservatee is Margaret O'Dowd, not Mike Zatopa. (4) The attorney for a conservator must not receive any payment from the estate of the conservatee in advance of an order of the court authorizing the payment. [CRC 7.755(a).] (5) The "Declaration in Response to Examiner Notes" filed on 4/21/23 was not signed or verified by the Court-appointed fiduciaries. [CRC 7.103(c).] (6) Exhibit F has numerous erroneous references to exhibits to the "underlying petition." (7) The childcare costs the parents are requesting to be reimbursed are inconsistent in paragraph 26 of their declaration attached as Exhibit F (as well as the figure used in paragraph 47 of the amended petition) and in paragraph 52 of this amended petition and paragraph 11 of the prayer. (8) Exhibit L shows numerous people who were paid for caregiving, including Nora Kimbrough and Eden Kimbrough at $30/hour, which information was not addressed in paragraphs 79 and 82 of the amended petition. (9) The first petition filed to establish a SNT was filed in a new trust case without the proper authority. The Court will have to determine if the time spent to prepare and file that petition was reasonable. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/1/23. Please do NOT file a fully amended pleading; either a supplement or an amendment, as appropriate, should be filed to address these issues. If an amendment to the amended petition is necessary to allege facts or request relief materially different from the facts alleged or the relief requested in the amended petition, and the amendment cannot be filed in time to give sufficient notice before the 6/12/23 hearing, Counsel should contact the assigned examiner to request a continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The "answers" set forth in the "Declaration in Response to Examiner Notes" filed on 4/21/23 should have been either incorporated into the amended petition or simply addressed, such as just mailing the correct Notice of Hearing; separate "Declaration" was unnecessary. The Court Investigator's Office is not a party required to be given notice.)

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/21/2023, pending settlement of the first account. A preliminary review indicates that the following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) No declaration re retention of the original statements of financial accounts has been filed. LRSF 14.59(A). A statement of each account showing the cash balance as of 12/31/2022 must be filed. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE COHOLAN FAMILY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 14, 1990 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/21/2023 (no earlier date available), as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) No declaration re retention of the original statement of each financial account verifying the ending cash balance is on file. See LRSF 14.59(A). 4) No statements appear to have been filed to verify the cash balance of the Credit Shelter Trust account and Special Needs Trust account as of 04/04/2021. 5) The allegations required by Probate Code section 1064 must be provided. 6) All beneficiaries must be identified in the petition. CRC 7.902. Such beneficiaries must include issue of Stephen Coholan, if any. 7) Please state whether Stephen Coholan has sufficient legal capacity without requiring a guardian ad litem. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CESAR ABREAU RIBEIRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) What kind of an asset is a deposit slip? (5) Detailed schedules means receipts must show the nature or purpose of each item, the source of the receipt, and the date thereof, not a lump sum from âvariousâ payors. [Probate Code Section 1062(a).] (6) What were the CPA fees for? (7) The probate referee's commission was $330.36, not $330.57. (8) The accounting is missing the additional schedule showing the estimated market value of the assets on hand as of the end of the accounting period. [Probate Code Section 1063(a).] (9) Who is the successor-in-interest to the post-deceased daughter Marion Ann Saput? Has an estate been opened for her or did she have a trust? That personal representative or trustee must be identified and must receive notice. (10) The executor is holding more than the FDIC insured amount in one bank. (11) If none of the other beneficiaries or heirs is willing to petition to be appointed as the administrator with will annexed, the executor might wish to nominate someone, such as a private professional fiduciary, attorney, or other professional, so that this estate administration is not unduly delayed. [Probate Code Section 8465(a).] After a successor administrator with will annexed is appointed, a second and final accounting by the executor will likely be required. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/23/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GARFIELD 1990 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendments Based On Undue Influence
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/24/2022) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) Please provide a copy of a Probate Code section 16061.7 notification served by the trustee of the Marital Trust. 3) The address of the trustee and each beneficiary must also be stated in the petition. Section 17201 & CRC 7.902. 4) Re Morton Garfield: i) Please provide his date of death. ii) If he survived Zalmon, the personal representative of Morton's estate must be identified and receive notice. Please also provide a copy of letters issued to the personal representative. If no probate administration is pending or anticipated, an allegation must be made as to whether Morton died testate or instate, and all devisees (if testate) and heirs (if intestate) must be identified and receive notice. If intestate, an allegation must also be made as to whether Morton was survived by a spouse or registered domestic partner. Section 17203(c) & CRC 7.51(e). iii) If Morton's specific gift has already been made, a copy of a signed receipt can be filed in lieu of a proof of service of notice. Sections 1206(b) & 17100. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAY CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amendment was filed on 3/27/23, but no courtesy copy has been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.]. (2) An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/23/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DAVID ALLAN HYMAN TRUST DATED 9/6/22
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Par. 14 of the petition purportedly lists " all heirs of the decedent" but no relatives are listed. 2) The assignment attached to the petition does not designate to whom the assignment is being made to. It is also signed by David Hyman as the trustee and not as an individual. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANDRA GRIFFIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/24/23. The following information must be supplemented seven days prior: (1) Need a declaration of due diligent search of the decedent's heirs at law. Petition merely states that decedent was not survived by no blood relatives. (2) Duties and Liabilities to be filed (3) Confidential Statement of Driver's License and Birth Date to be filed. (4) Proposed Order must be submitted. (5) If surviving spouse is the only successor in interest, if administration is needed.
